2604.12843.title: Growing Pains: Extensible and Efficient LLM Benchmarking Via Fixed Parameter Calibration
2604.12843.paper_summary: The rapid release of both language models and benchmarks makes it increasingly costly to evaluate every model on every dataset. In practice, models are often evaluated on different samples, making scores difficult to compare across studies. To address this, we propose a framework based on multidimensional Item Response Theory (IRT) that uses anchor items to calibrate new benchmarks to the evaluation suite while holding previously calibrated item parameters fixed. Our approach supports a realistic evaluation setting in which datasets are introduced over time and models are evaluated only on the datasets available at the time of evaluation, while a fixed anchor set for each dataset is used so that results from different evaluation periods can be compared directly. In large-scale experiments on more than $400$ models, our framework predicts full-evaluation performance within 2-3 percentage points using only $100$ anchor questions per dataset, with Spearman $ρ\geq 0.9$ for ranking preservation, showing that it is possible to extend benchmark suites over time while preserving score comparability, at a constant evaluation cost per new dataset. Code available at https://github.com/eliyahabba/growing-pains
2604.12843.paper_summary_zh: 快速發布的語言模型和基準使得在每個數據集上評估每個模型的成本越來越高。<lb/>在實踐中，模型通常在不同的樣本上進行評估，這使得跨研究的分數比較變得困難。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一個基於多維項目反應理論（IRT）的框架，該框架利用錨定項目來校準新的基準到評估套件，同時保持之前校準的項目參數不變。<lb/>我們的方法支持一個現實的評估環境，在這個環境中，數據集隨時間引入，模型僅在評估時可用的數據集上進行評估，同時對每個數據集使用固定的錨定集，以便能夠直接比較不同評估時期的結果。<lb/>在對超過 $400$ 個模型的大規模實驗中，我們的框架僅使用每個數據集 $100$ 個錨定問題，就能在 $2-3$ 個百分點內預測完整評估性能，並且在排名保持方面的斯皮爾曼 $ρ\geq 0.9$，顯示出隨著時間推移擴展基準套件同時保持分數可比較性是可能的，且每個新數據集的評估成本保持不變。<lb/>代碼可在 https://github.com/eliyahabba/growing-pains 獲得。
2604.12843.author: Eliya Habba et.al.
2604.12843.authors: Eliya Habba, Itay Itzhak, Asaf Yehudai, Yotam Perlitz, Elron Bandel, Michal Shmueli-Scheuer, Leshem Choshen, Gabriel Stanovsky
